phase1_details.reason: Both involve defining/looking up a reusable metric formula in a cloud infrastructure/capacity planning context. The core scenario — creating and verifying a metric definition for team use — is essentially the same, with dana_009 adding a trivial 'look it up to confirm' step that doesn't meaningfully differentiate the conversation.

Both involve fixing a column in an imported dataset where values were stored in the wrong format — dana_000 is about inconsistently formatted text labels in AWS tags, dana_002 is about date strings imported as text. However, these are actually different data problems (string normalization vs. type casting), so I will not flag these as duplicates.

Both are lifestyle/wellness bloggers validating that column values conform to an allowed list before publishing stats to readers. The specific domain differs slightly (budget categories vs. fitness check-ins), but the scenario structure — blogger + validate values against fixed allowed set + share with audience — is essentially interchangeable.

Both are hobby/lifestyle bloggers cleaning a personal spreadsheet where date or measurement fields have inconsistent formats that are breaking a chart, and need values cast correctly and out-of-range entries flagged before publishing. The specific domain (cooking vs. gardening) does not differentiate the core conversation meaningfully.
